preview_text:
The model favours Argentina at 80%, with Jordan assigned a 4% win probability. The draw carries 16% likelihood, positioning it as a meaningful secondary outcome relative to a Jordan upset.

The spread reflects a heavily lopsided matchup. Argentina's win probability dominates by 76 percentage points over Jordan's chance, yet the draw's material probability—nearly one in six—suggests the match carries competitive structure rather than forming a foregone conclusion. A low-scoring stalemate or defensive performance from the underdog could materialise within the model's distribution.

Model and sportsbook are closely aligned across all three outcomes. The consensus places Argentina at 79%, only 1 percentage point below the model, while Jordan sits 3 points higher at 7% and the draw 2 points lower at 14%. These gaps fall within typical variance across book samples and reflect broad agreement on Argentina as the heavy favourite.
